notes: DEC's super-fast backup/restore solution; 400-750 GB/hour; AlphaServer 8400 + tape automation. Discontinued with DEC acquisition by Compaq in 1998.
8-CPU Alpha server used as NonStop VLDB backup server hardware. Benchmark configuration: 8 CPUs, 8 GB RAM, 30 SCSI controllers, 16 tape drives.
DEC's high-speed I/O channel supporting 4 PCI buses; key enabler of NonStop VLDB performance.
The 'major backup technology advance of the last 10 years' per Aberdeen in 1997. Evolved into SAN snapshots, deduplication, and cloud backup.
SAP R/3 enterprise application compatible with NonStop VLDB; backup results 400-750 GB/hour. Succeeded by SAP S/4HANA.
Tested with NonStop VLDB. Legato acquired by EMC 2003; NetWorker continues as Dell Technologies NetWorker.
Tested with NonStop VLDB. Cheyenne acquired by CA 1996; ARCserve continues as Arcserve (spun out 2019).
OS for AlphaServer NonStop VLDB configurations. Support ended ~2012.
Technology that Aberdeen's vision of fast backup/restore foreshadowed. Data Domain (EMC) pioneered deduplication in early 2000s; now ubiquitous in backup.
The ultimate realization of Aberdeen's 'backup goes strategic' vision; cloud backup/DR as service now standard enterprise practice.
